content:
At one point, I realized something uncomfortable.
Most of my day wasn’t spent building interesting things.
It was spent doing the same tasks over and over:

• Reading emails

• Summarizing requests

• Writing replies

• Updating spreadsheets

• Generating reports
None of it was hard.
It was just repetitive.
So I did what any developer would do.
I tried to automate it.
What started as a personal productivity experiment turned into something far more interesting:
A reusable AI workflow that businesses were willing to pay for — not because they couldn’t build it, but because they didn’t want to maintain it.
These are the tools, libraries, and patterns that made that system work.
1. LangGraph Turned My Messy Scripts Into…
